merged_compliance.pass_knowledge_template: Device {device_name} satisfies all configured network audit rules: loop detection, single point of failure analysis, VLAN consistency validation, and star topology verification.
merged_compliance.global_pass_summary: All configured network audit rules passed. The topology is compliant with loop, resilience, VLAN, and star topology requirements.
merged_compliance.global_fail_summary: One or more network audit rules reported violations. Review node-level compliance_status and audit_knowledge properties for remediation guidance.

global_summary.pass: Layer-two loop detection passed. No circular physical paths were identified in the topology graph.
global_summary.fail: Layer-two loop detection failed. One or more circular paths exist and may cause broadcast storms or MAC table instability.

node_knowledge_template.pass: Device {device_name} is not participating in any detected layer-two loop. All adjacent links form an acyclic spanning structure at this node.
node_knowledge_template.fail: Device {device_name} participates in a layer-two loop. Circular path: {cycle_path}. All devices in the cycle: {cycle_devices}.

global_summary.pass: Single point of failure analysis passed. No articulation points were identified that would partition the network upon device failure.
global_summary.fail: Single point of failure analysis failed. One or more devices are articulation points whose failure would isolate network segments.

node_knowledge_template.pass: Device {device_name} is not a single point of failure. Removing this device would not partition the remaining topology graph.
node_knowledge_template.fail: Device {device_name} is a single point of failure (articulation point). Failure or maintenance on this device will isolate connected network segments. Adjacent devices: {adjacent_devices}.

global_summary.pass: VLAN consistency validation passed. All cabled port pairs report matching VLAN identifiers on both ends.
global_summary.fail: VLAN consistency validation failed. One or more cabled port pairs terminate on ports assigned to different VLAN identifiers.

node_knowledge_template.pass: Device {device_name} has no detected VLAN mismatches on any cabled interface participating in the topology graph.
node_knowledge_template.fail: Device {device_name} has a VLAN mismatch on cabled interface {local_interface} connected to {peer_device} interface {peer_interface}. Local VLAN ID: {local_vlan_id}. Peer VLAN ID: {peer_vlan_id}. Cable ID: {cable_id}.

vlan_mismatch.link_knowledge_template: Physical link between {source_device} and {target_device} has mismatched VLAN assignments. {source_device} interface {source_interface} uses VLAN {source_vlan_id} while {target_device} interface {target_interface} uses VLAN {target_vlan_id}.

global_summary.pass: Star topology validation passed. The graph contains exactly one central hub with degree N-1 and all peripheral nodes have degree 1.
global_summary.fail: Star topology validation failed. The graph does not conform to strict star specifications.

node_knowledge_template.pass: Device {device_name} conforms to strict star topology requirements. Node degree: {node_degree}. Expected role: {expected_role}.
node_knowledge_template.hub_missing: Device {device_name} cannot conform to star topology because no valid central hub exists. Node degree: {node_degree}. A star requires exactly one hub with degree {required_hub_degree}.
node_knowledge_template.hub_invalid: Device {device_name} is designated as the central hub but its degree is {node_degree}. A valid hub must have degree {required_hub_degree} (N-1) for a graph with {node_count} devices.
node_knowledge_template.multiple_hubs: Device {device_name} qualifies as a central hub candidate but multiple hub candidates were detected: {hub_candidates}. A strict star allows exactly one hub.
node_knowledge_template.peripheral_invalid: Device {device_name} is a peripheral node but its degree is {node_degree}. Peripheral nodes in a strict star must have degree exactly {required_peripheral_degree}. Additional links indicate illegal cross-connections or mesh paths.
node_knowledge_template.peripheral_pass: Device {device_name} is a valid peripheral node in the star topology with degree {node_degree}, connected only to the central hub {hub_device}.
